tipRules.tip: Palaniuk: 'In October, fish a 1/2-ounce flipping jig around isolated cover in the backs of creeks and adjacent to channel swings with big flats. Target laydowns, single docks, shallow brushpiles, or any isolated targets.'

knowledge.insight: Palaniuk: 'In fall, cooling temps trigger bass to feed heavily for winter and the spawn. Bass prefer to eat a bigger bait so they dont have to chase as much small bait. A big swimbait moves slowly through the water column so bass can track it easier. Thats why I start with a giant swimbait in October.'
Palaniuk: 'In fall, bigger bass set up to ambush bait on isolated cover in creek backs and adjacent to channel swings with big flats. Match jig color to water color.'
Palaniuk: 'For flipping jig in fall: 7-foot-6 heavy-action Alpha Angler Hitter rod, Daiwa Steez A reel, 20-pound Seaguar Tatsu fluorocarbon line.'
